2604.12816.title: The role of System 1 and System 2 semantic memory structure in human and LLM biases
2604.12816.paper_summary: Implicit biases in both humans and large language models (LLMs) pose significant societal risks. Dual process theories propose that biases arise primarily from associative System 1 thinking, while deliberative System 2 thinking mitigates bias, but the cognitive mechanisms that give rise to this phenomenon remain poorly understood. To better understand what underlies this duality in humans, and possibly in LLMs, we model System 1 and System 2 thinking as semantic memory networks with distinct structures, built from comparable datasets generated by both humans and LLMs. We then investigate how these distinct semantic memory structures relate to implicit gender bias using network-based evaluation metrics. We find that semantic memory structures are irreducible only in humans, suggesting that LLMs lack certain types of human-like conceptual knowledge. Moreover, semantic memory structure relates consistently to implicit bias only in humans, with lower levels of bias in System~2 structures. These findings suggest that certain types of conceptual knowledge contribute to bias regulation in humans, but not in LLMs, highlighting fundamental differences between human and machine cognition.
2604.12816.paper_summary_zh: 隱性偏見在人體和大型語言模型（LLMs）中都構成了重大的社會風險。雙重過程理論提出，偏見主要源自聯想性的系統1思維，而深思熟慮的系統2思維則能減輕偏見，但導致這一現象的認知機制仍然不甚了解。為了更好地理解這種二元性在人體中的根源，以及可能在LLMs中的根源，我們將系統1和系統2思維建模為具有不同結構的語義記憶網絡，這些結構是基於人類和LLMs生成的可比較數據集構建的。我們接著調查這些不同的語義記憶結構如何與隱性性別偏見相關，使用基於網絡的評估指標。我們發現語義記憶結構僅在人體中是不可簡約的，這表明LLMs缺乏某些類型的人類概念知識。此外，語義記憶結構僅在人類中與隱性偏見一致相關，系統2結構中的偏見水平較低。這些發現表明，某些類型的概念知識有助於人類的偏見調節，但對LLMs則不然，突顯了人類與機器認知之間的根本差異。
2604.12816.author: Katherine Abramski et.al.
2604.12816.authors: Katherine Abramski, Giulio Rossetti, Massimo Stella
